title: Comment Your Code
prompt_md:
## Goal

Add comments to a working program so that another programmer can understand its purpose.

## Input

This program takes no input.

## Output

The program returns `0` from `main`. Adding comments must not change the return value.

Add a **multi-line comment** (`/* ... */`) at the top of the file (before `int main()`) describing what the program does (at least two lines of text), and a **single-line comment** (`//`) above the `return` statement explaining what returning `0` means.

title: Commented-Out Code
prompt_md:
## Goal

Use comments to change which print statement actually executes, without deleting any line.

## Input

This program takes no input.

## Output

The program must print `42` on its own line.

The starter code has two print statements; currently it prints `0`. Comment out the line that prints `0` and uncomment the line that prints `42` — do not delete or add any lines, only add or remove `//` markers.
